verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 (TGN46) is a type I single-pass integral membrane protein whose dominant localization is the trans-Golgi network, but it constitutively cycles between the TGN, endosomes, and the plasma membrane via its own TM anchor. When at the cell surface, its large lumenal/extracellular domain is exposed on the outer leaflet. The PM pool is a minor, transient fraction; the steady-state bulk resides in the endomembrane. This dual localization with documented, intrinsic surface transit qualifies as contextual.
